name: healthcare_policy_eval_v1
description: Initial evaluation set for the six synthetic policy documents. Mix of direct factual lookups, paraphrases, identifier-based queries, and one out-of-scope question to verify the refusal path. Synthetic data only.

items.question: How long do I have to file a first-level appeal?
items.expected_answer: Within 60 calendar days of the denial notice.

items.question: How fast must an expedited appeal be decided?
How many days does an external Independent Review Organization have to issue a decision?
items.expected_answer: Within 45 calendar days of the IRO's receipt of the request.

items.question: What does denial code HF-022 mean?
items.expected_answer: Prior authorization was required but not on file at the time of service.

items.question: How long does a provider have to request internal review of a claim denial?
items.expected_answer: Within 30 calendar days of the Remittance Advice date.

items.question: Which imaging services require prior authorization?
items.expected_answer: MRI, CT, PET, and nuclear cardiology studies.

items.question: What is the decision timeline for an urgent prior authorization?
What is the standard prior authorization review timeline?
items.expected_answer: Within 5 business days of receipt of a complete request.

items.question: Does the HIPAA minimum necessary standard apply when disclosing PHI to another provider for treatment?
items.expected_answer: No. The minimum necessary standard does not apply to disclosures for treatment.

items.question: How long does the Privacy Office have to approve or deny a non-routine PHI request?
Can a provider balance bill a member for emergency services rendered out-of-network?
items.expected_answer: No. Members are held harmless from balance billing for emergency services and pay in-network cost-sharing.

items.question: What is the timely filing window for out-of-network claims?
items.expected_answer: Within 180 calendar days of the date of service.

items.question: What modifier is required for synchronous audio-video telehealth visits?
What place-of-service code is used when a member receives telehealth at home?
Are audio-only behavioral health telehealth visits reimbursed at parity?
items.expected_answer: Yes. Audio-only behavioral health services are reimbursed at parity with audio-visual telehealth.

items.question: What is the capital of France?
Should I take ibuprofen or acetaminophen for my headache?
